typstR

typstR gives research and policy teams a working-paper-first path from Quarto source to a polished Typst PDF. The package ships scaffold helpers, publication metadata helpers, and bundled templates so you can stay in a normal Quarto workflow while using a publication-ready format.

Start here

Install the package in R:

remotes::install_github("DavidZenz/typstR")

You can install typstR immediately in R. Quarto is only required when you render a document.

The default onboarding path is:

library(typstR)

create_working_paper("my-paper")
# edit my-paper/template.qmd
render_working_paper("my-paper/template.qmd")

That single working-paper flow is the canonical first run for the package.

What to edit after scaffolding

create_working_paper() creates four key pieces:

  • template.qmd for the manuscript body and YAML front matter
  • _quarto.yml for project-level Quarto settings
  • references.bib for citations
  • _extensions/typstR/ for the bundled format extension

If you want the full walkthrough of how those files fit together, start with Getting started.

Where to go next

If you need… Start here
The full install -> scaffold -> render walkthrough Getting started
More detail on title-page metadata and working-paper fields Working Papers
Practical YAML knobs for logos, fonts, and color Customizing Branding
Function-level help for scaffold and render wrappers Reference

Other format entry points

Once the working-paper path is clear, you can branch into adjacent formats:

create_article("my-article")
create_policy_brief("my-brief")

Use create_article() for article-style manuscripts and create_policy_brief() for shorter policy-facing outputs.
